fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the item.

fields.name: No. 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies the alternative number of the item.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a description of the item.

fields.name: Search Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a search description that you use to find the item in lists.

fields.name: Description 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies information in addition to the description.

fields.name: Assembly BOM
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the item is an assembly BOM.

fields.name: Base Unit of Measure
fields.tooltip: Specifies the unit in which the item is held in inventory.

fields.name: Price Unit Conversion
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the price unit conversion for the item.
The field is not used elsewhere in the program.

fields.name: Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the item card represents a physical inventory unit (Inventory), a labor time unit (Service), or a physical unit that is not tracked in inventory (Non-Inventory).

fields.name: Inventory Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies links between business transactions made for the item and an inventory account in the general ledger, to group amounts for that item type.

fields.name: Shelf No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies where to find the item in the warehouse. This is informational only.

fields.name: Item Disc. Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies an item group code that can be used as a criterion to grant a discount when the item is sold to a certain customer.

fields.name: Allow Invoice Disc.
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the item should be included in the calculation of an invoice discount on documents where the item is traded.

fields.name: Statistics Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the statistics group.

fields.name: Commission Group
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a code that can be used, for example, to report on items that trigger sales commission. The field is strictly informational and not related to any commission functionality.
Example
A basic use of the field could be to enter a code in this field for items that trigger a sales commission. In any item report, such as Inventory - Customer Sales, add it as a filter field to view all item sales that warrant the sales commission.

fields.name: Unit Price
fields.tooltip: Specifies the price for one unit of the item, in LCY.

fields.name: Price/Profit Calculation
fields.tooltip: Specifies the relationship between the Unit Cost, Unit Price, and Profit Percentage fields associated with this item.

fields.name: Profit %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the profit margin that you want to sell the item at. You can enter a profit percentage manually or have it entered according to the Price/Profit Calculation field

fields.name: Costing Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies the method that is used to calculate the unit cost of the item.

fields.name: Unit Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the cost of one unit of the item.

fields.name: Standard Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the unit cost that is used as an estimation to be adjusted with variances later.

fields.name: Last Direct Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the most recent direct unit cost that was paid for the item.

fields.name: Indirect Cost %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage of the item's last purchase cost that includes indirect costs, such as freight that is associated with the purchase of the item.

fields.name: Cost is Adjusted
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the cost of the item has been adjusted.

fields.name: Allow Online Adjustment
fields.tooltip:
Specifies if the items unit cost will be adjusted automatically.
It is used internally in relation to the Automatic Cost Adjustment setup field.

fields.name: Vendor No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the vendor from whom this item is supplied by default.

fields.name: Vendor Item No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number that the vendor uses for this item.

fields.name: Lead Time Calculation
fields.tooltip: Specifies a date formula for the amount of time it takes to replenish the item.

fields.name: Reorder Point
fields.tooltip: Specifies a stock quantity that sets the inventory below the level that you must replenish the item.

fields.name: Maximum Inventory
fields.tooltip: Specifies a quantity that you want to use as a maximum inventory level.

fields.name: Reorder Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies a standard lot size quantity to be used for all order proposals.

fields.name: Alternative Item No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies an alternative number for the item.

fields.name: Unit List Price
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a recommended unit price.
This field is not used elsewhere in the program.

fields.name: Duty Due %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage of the item on which duty will be assessed. Do not enter the percent sign. For example, if the duty % is 5.5%, enter 5.5.

fields.name: Duty Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a code for the applicable duty.

fields.name: Gross Weight
fields.tooltip: Specifies the gross weight of the item.

fields.name: Net Weight
fields.tooltip: Specifies the net weight of the item.

fields.name: Units per Parcel
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of units per parcel of the item.
You may need the number of units per parcel when you fill out customs documents, waybills, and so on.

fields.name: Unit Volume
fields.tooltip: Specifies the volume of one unit of the item.

fields.name: Durability
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the durability of the item. You can enter a maximum of 10 characters, both numbers and letters.
You may need to know the durability to complete customs documents, waybills, and so on.

fields.name: Freight Type
fields.tooltip:
Specifies how the item is shipped. You can enter a maximum of 10 characters, both numbers and letters.
You may need to know the freight type to complete shipping documents.

fields.name: Tariff No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a code for the item's tariff number.

fields.name: Duty Unit Conversion
fields.tooltip: Specifies a factor that gives the relationship between the unit used by the customs and tax authorities for the item and the unit used by your company.

fields.name: Country/Region Purchased Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a code for the country/region in which the item is purchased. To see the country/region codes in the Country/Region table, choose the field.

fields.name: Budget Quantity
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a budget figure for the quantity of items sold.
The Budget Quantity field must be updated manually and has no relationship to any budgets in the general ledger.

fields.name: Budgeted Amount
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a budget figure for the total amount of items sold.
The Budgeted Amount field must be updated manually and has no relationship to any budgets in the general ledger.

fields.name: Budget Profit
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a budget figure for the total profit on the items sold.
The Budget Profit field must be updated manually and has no relationship to any budgets in the general ledger.

fields.name: Comment
fields.tooltip:
Shows whether there is a comment for this item.
Comments could concern a particular storage method or substitute items.
You can also enter a comment in the Notes FactBox.

fields.name: Blocked
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the related record is blocked from being posted in transactions, for example an item that is placed in quarantine.

fields.name: Cost is Posted to G/L
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the cost of the item has been posted to the general ledger.

fields.name: Last Date Modified
fields.tooltip: Specifies when the item card was last modified.

fields.name: Date Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the date interval that values are filtered by.
If this field contains starting and ending dates for a date interval, then other field values only show information for that interval.
For more information, see How to: Enter Dates and Times.

fields.name: Global Dimension 1 Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a filter for a dimension. This means that values in the fields that contain quantities are based only on entries with the dimension values included in the filter.
To see the dimension values you have set up for a dimension in the Dimension Value table, choose the field.
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: Global Dimension 2 Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a filter for a dimension. This means that values in the fields that contain quantities are based only on entries with the dimension values included in the filter.
To see the dimension values you have set up for a dimension in the Dimension Value table, choose the field.
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: Location Filter
fields.tooltip:
Displays the filter that filters by location.
If the field contains a location code, the values in the fields that contain quantities are based only on entries with the location code that is included in the filter.
You can enter a maximum of 10 characters, both numbers and letters. There are certain rules for how these can be combined:

Meaning
Sample Expression
Included

Equal to

Oslo

Entries from the Oslo location


Interval

1..5

Entries from Locations 1 through 5


Either/or

Oslo|Vienna

Entries that are from either Oslo or Vienna


Different from

<>1

Entries from all locations except Location 1


To see the location codes in the Location table, choose the field.

fields.name: Inventory
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total quantity of the item that is currently in inventory at all locations.

fields.name: Net Invoiced Qty.
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item in inventory have been invoiced.

fields.name: Net Change
fields.tooltip:
Shows the net change in the inventory of the item during the period entered in the Date Filter field.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using the Quantity field in the Item Ledger Entry table.
You can filter the Net Change field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of one or any combination of the following:
Global Dimension 1 values

Global Dimension 2 values

locations

item variants


lot numbers


serial numbers


You can also set a filter in the Drop Shipment Filter field so that the contents of the Net Change field are calculated on the basis of the items that were shipped as a drop shipment.
To see the item ledger entries that make up the number shown, choose the field.

fields.name: Purchases (Qty.)
fields.tooltip:
Shows how many units of the item have been purchased.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field, using the Invoiced Quantity field in the Item Ledger Entry table for those entries of the Purchase type.
You can filter the Purchases (Qty.) field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of one or any combination of the following:
Global Dimension 1 values

Global Dimension 2 values

locations

item variants

posting dates


lot numbers


serial numbers


You can also set a filter in the Drop Shipment Filter field so that the contents of the Purchases (Qty.) field are calculated on the basis of the items that were shipped as a drop shipment.
To see the item ledger entries that make up the number shown, choose the field.

fields.name: Sales (Qty.)
fields.tooltip:
Shows how many units of the item have been sold.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field, using the Invoiced Quantity field in the Item Ledger Entry table for those entries of the Sales type.
You can filter the Sales (Qty.) field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of one or any combination of the following:
Global Dimension 1 values

Global Dimension 2 values

locations

item variants

dates


You can also set a filter in the Drop Shipment Filter field so that the contents of the Sales (Qty.) field are calculated on the basis of the items that were shipped as a drop shipment.
To see the item ledger entries that make up the number shown, choose the field.

fields.name: Positive Adjmt. (Qty.)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total number of units that have been added to the inventory without being purchased.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field, using the Invoiced Quantity field in the Item Ledger Entry table for those entries of the Positive Adj. type.
You can filter the Positive Adjmt. (Qty.) field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of one or any combination of the following:
Global Dimension 1 values

Global Dimension 2 values

locations

item variants

dates


lot numbers


serial numbers


You can also set a filter in the Drop Shipment Filter field so that the contents of the Positive Adjmt. (Qty.) field are calculated on the basis of the items that were ordered as a drop shipment.
To see the item ledger entries that make up the number shown, choose the field.

fields.name: Negative Adjmt. (Qty.)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total number of units that have been removed from the inventory without being sold.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field, using the Invoiced Quantity field in the Item Ledger Entry table for those entries of the Negative Adj. type.
You can filter the Negative Adjmt. (Qty.) field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of one or any combination of the following:
Global Dimension 1 values

Global Dimension 2 values

locations

item variants

dates


To see the item ledger entries that make up the number shown, choose the field.

fields.name: Purchases (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Contains the total purchase amount, in LCY, of the item that has been purchased.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field, using the Sales Amount (Actual) field in the Value Entry table for those entries that have been posted as purchases.
You can filter the Purchases (LCY) field so that its contents are calculated only on the basis of one or any combination of the following:
Global Dimension 1 values

Global Dimension 2 values

dates

locations

item variants


You can also set a filter in the Drop Shipment Filter field so that the contents of the Purchases (LCY) field are calculated on the basis of the items that were ordered as a drop shipment.

fields.name: Sales (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Contains the sales amount, in LCY, of the item that has been sold.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field, using the Sales Amount (Actual) field in the Value Entry table for those entries that have been posted as sales.
You can filter the Sales (LCY) field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of one or any combination of the following:
Global Dimension 1 values

Global Dimension 2 values

locations

dates

item variants


You can also set a filter in the Drop Shipment Filter field so that the contents of the Sales (LCY) field are calculated on the basis of the items that were shipped as a drop shipment.

fields.name: Positive Adjmt. (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Contains the total value of the units that have been added to inventory without being purchased.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field, using the Sales Amount (Actual) field in the Value Entry table for those entries that have been posted as positive adjustments.
You can filter the Positive Adjmt. (LCY) field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of one or any combination of the following:
Global Dimension 1 values

Global Dimension 2 values

dates

locations

item variants


You can also set a filter in the Drop Shipment Filter field so that the contents of the Positive Adjmt. (LCY) field are calculated on the basis of the items that will be shipped as a drop shipment.

fields.name: Negative Adjmt. (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Contains the total value of units that have been removed from inventory without being sold.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field, using the Sales Amount (Actual) field in the Value Entry table for those entries that have been posted as negative adjustments.
You can filter the Negative Adjmt. (LCY) field so that its contents are calculated only on the basis of one or any combination of the following:
Global Dimension 1 values

Global Dimension 2 values

dates

locations

item variants


You can also set a filter in the Drop Shipment Filter field so that the contents of the Negative Adjmt. (LCY) field are calculated on the basis of the items that were ordered as a drop shipment.

fields.name: COGS (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Contains the total cost of goods sold for items that you have shipped from inventory and invoiced.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field, using the Cost Amount (Actual) field in the Value Entry table for those entries that have been posted as sales.
You can filter the COGS (LCY) field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of one or any combination of the following:
Global Dimension 1 values

Global Dimension 2 values

dates

locations

item variants


You can also set a filter in the Drop Shipment Filter field so that the contents of the COGS (LCY) field are calculated on the basis of the items that were shipped as a drop shipment.

fields.name: Qty. on Purch. Order
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item are inbound on purchase orders, meaning listed on outstanding purchase order lines.

fields.name: Qty. on Sales Order
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item are allocated to sales orders, meaning listed on outstanding sales orders lines.

fields.name: Price Includes VAT
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the Unit Price and Line Amount fields on sales document lines for this item should be shown with or without VAT.

fields.name: Drop Shipment Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether to put a drop shipment filter on the item card.
If you put a drop shipment filter on the card, the values in the fields that show quantities are based only on entries for items that are shipped directly from the vendor to the customer (that is, as a drop shipment).

fields.name: VAT Bus. Posting Gr. (Price)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT business posting group for customers for whom you want the sales price including VAT to apply.

fields.name: Gen. Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the item's product type to link transactions made for this item with the appropriate general ledger account according to the general posting setup.

fields.name: Picture
fields.tooltip: Specifies the picture that has been inserted for the item.

fields.name: Transferred (Qty.)
fields.tooltip:
Shows how many units of the item that are transferred from one location or dimension value to another.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field, using the Invoiced Quantity field in the Item Ledger Entry table for those entries of the Transfer type.
You can filter the Transfer (Qty.) field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of one or any combination of the following:
Global Dimension 1 values

Global Dimension 2 values

dates

locations

item variants


lot numbers


serial numbers


To see the item ledger entries that make up the number shown, choose the field.

fields.name: Transferred (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Contains the total amount, in LCY, of the items that have been transferred from one location or dimension value to another.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field, using the Sales Amount (Actual) field in the Value Entry table for those entries that have been posted as transfers.
You can filter the Transferred (LCY) field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of one or any combination of the following:
Global Dimension 1 values

Global Dimension 2 values

dates

locations

item variants


You can also set a filter in the Drop Shipment Filter field so that the contents of the Transferred (LCY) field are calculated on the basis of the items that will be shipped as a drop shipment.

fields.name: Country/Region of Origin Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a code for the country/region where the item was produced or processed.

fields.name: Automatic Ext. Texts
fields.tooltip: Specifies that an extended text that you have set up will be added automatically on sales or purchase documents for this item.

fields.name: No. Series
fields.tooltip: Contains the code for the number series that was used to assign the item's number.

fields.name: Tax Group Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the tax group that is used to calculate and post sales tax.

fields.name: VAT Prod. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT specification of the involved item or resource to link transactions made for this record with the appropriate general ledger account according to the VAT posting setup.

fields.name: Reserve
fields.tooltip: Specifies if and how the item will be reserved. Never: It is not possible to reserve the item. Optional: You can reserve the item manually. Always: The item is automatically reserved from demand, such as sales orders, against inventory, purchase orders, assembly orders, and production orders.

fields.name: Reserved Qty. on Inventory
fields.tooltip:
Indicates the quantity of the item that is reserved from inventory.
Choose the field to see the reservation entries that make up the quantity.

fields.name: Reserved Qty. on Purch. Orders
fields.tooltip:
Indicates the quantity of the item that is reserved from purchase orders.
Choose the field to see the reservation entries that make up the quantity.

fields.name: Reserved Qty. on Sales Orders
fields.tooltip:
Indicates the quantity of the item that is reserved from sales orders.
Choose the field to see the reservation entries that make up the quantity.

fields.name: Global Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a dimension value code. To see the dimension value codes that you have set up for this dimension in the Dimension Value window, choose the field.
If you enter a dimension value code in this field, then the corresponding dimension value is used as the default value every time that you enter the account on an entry line. Default dimension values are only suggestions, and you can change them on the entry line, such as in a journal.

fields.name: Global Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a dimension value code. To see the dimension value codes that you have set up for this dimension in the Dimension Value window, click the lookup button in the field.
If you enter a dimension value code in this field, then the corresponding dimension value is used as the default value every time that you enter the account on an entry line. Default dimension values are only suggestions, and you can change them on the entry line, such as in a journal.

fields.name: Res. Qty. on Outbound Transfer
fields.tooltip:
Indicates the quantity of the item that is reserved.
Choose the field to see the reservation entries that make up the quantity.

fields.name: Res. Qty. on Inbound Transfer
fields.tooltip:
Indicates the quantity of the item that is reserved.
Choose the field to see the reservation entries that make up the quantity.

fields.name: Stockout Warning
fields.tooltip: Specifies if a warning is displayed when you enter a quantity on a sales document that brings the item's inventory below zero.

fields.name: Prevent Negative Inventory
fields.tooltip: Specifies if you can post a transaction that will bring the item's inventory below zero.

fields.name: Variant Mandatory if Exists
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether a variant must be selected if variants exist for the item.

fields.name: Application Wksh. User ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ID of a user who is working in the Application Worksheet window.

fields.name: Coupled to Dataverse
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the item is coupled to a product in Dynamics 365 Sales.

fields.name: Assembly Policy
fields.tooltip: Specifies which default order flow is used to supply this assembly item.

fields.name: Qty. on Job Order
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item are allocated to projects, meaning listed on outstanding project planning lines.

fields.name: Res. Qty. on Job Order
fields.tooltip:
Indicates the quantity of the item that is reserved for jobs.
Click the look-up button to the right of the field to see the reservation entries that make up the quantity.

fields.name: GTIN
fields.tooltip: Specifies the Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) for the item. For example, the GTIN is used with bar codes to track items, and when sending and receiving documents electronically. The GTIN number typically contains a Universal Product Code (UPC), or European Article Number (EAN).

fields.name: Default Deferral Template Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how revenue or expenses for the item are deferred to other accounting periods by default.

fields.name: Low-Level Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the item's level in a bill of material if the item is a component in a production BOM or an assembly BOM.

fields.name: Lot Size
fields.tooltip:
Specifies how many units of the item are processed in one production operation by default.
You can modify the lot size on individual routing lines or on individual production order routing lines (operations).

Additional Information

The lot size modifies the run time that you enter on routing lines by multiplying the value in the Run Time field with the lot size to reflect that it takes, for example, twice the time to process two pieces per operation.
Note


Note that, although it is not a productive time element, the value in the Setup Time field on routing lines is also multiplied by the lot size.


If the item routing includes fixed costs such as setup time, then the value in this field is used to calculate the standard cost price and distribute the fixed costs of manufacturing the item.
To limit the amount of planning suggestions for insignificant changes to existing orders, use the Default Dampener Quantity field, which is a percentage of the lot size.

fields.name: Serial Nos.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a number series code to assign consecutive serial numbers to items produced.

fields.name: Last Unit Cost Calc. Date
fields.tooltip: Shows the date on which you ran the Calculate Standard Cost batch job last.

fields.name: Rolled-up Material Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total material cost of all the items at all levels of the items BOM, added to the material cost of the item itself.

fields.name: Rolled-up Capacity Cost
fields.tooltip: Contains the rolled-up capacity cost for the item. This is a calculation of the total capacity cost for all BOM levels of the components that make up the production BOM added to the material cost of the parent item.

fields.name: Scrap %
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the percentage of the item that you expect to be scrapped in the production process. It is used to calculate unit prices and net requirements.
If the item is a parent, then the scrap percentage represents scrapped output before putting away in inventory. It the item is a component, then the scrap percentage represents scrapped consumption, when picking from inventory.
If you define a scrap percentage for a component item, it means that more components will be withdrawn from inventory than the production order calls for.

fields.name: Rolled-up Mat. Non-Invt. Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the Non-inventory material cost of all items at all levels of the parent item's BOM.

fields.name: Inventory Value Zero
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the item on inventory must be excluded from inventory valuation. This is relevant if the item is kept on inventory on someone else's behalf.

fields.name: Discrete Order Quantity
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Minimum Order Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies a minimum allowable quantity for an item order proposal.

fields.name: Maximum Order Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies a maximum allowable quantity for an item order proposal.

fields.name: Safety Stock Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies a quantity of stock to have in inventory to protect against supply-and-demand fluctuations during replenishment lead time.

fields.name: Order Multiple
fields.tooltip: Specifies a parameter used by the planning system to round the quantity of planned supply orders to a multiple of this value.

fields.name: Safety Lead Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies a date formula to indicate a safety lead time that can be used as a buffer period for production and other delays.

fields.name: Flushing Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies how consumption of the item (component) is calculated and handled in production processes. Manual: Enter and post consumption in the consumption journal manually. Forward: Automatically posts consumption according to the production order component lines when the first operation starts. Backward: Automatically calculates and posts consumption according to the production order component lines when the production order is finished. Pick + Forward / Pick + Backward: Variations with warehousing.

fields.name: Replenishment System
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of supply order that is created by the planning system when the item needs to be replenished.

fields.name: Rounding Precision
fields.tooltip:
Defines how calculated consumption quantities are rounded when entered on consumption journal lines. Quantities less than 0.5 will be rounded down. Quantities equal to or greater than 0.5 will be rounded up.
You can set the field to round to the nearest decimal (maximum 6 decimal places) or to the nearest number divisible by any whole or decimal number.
Examples
0.01 Round to two decimals
0.05 Round to a number divisible by 0.05
1.00 Round to a whole number (no decimals - divisible by 1.00)
10.00 Round to a whole number (no decimals - divisible by 10.00)


Additional Information

Changing the field only affects future consumption calculations.
The rounding precision only applies to the unit of measure that the item uses on the production order component and is later carried to the consumption journal. If the item has two units of measure, such as grams and kilograms, then the item's rounding precision applies in the same way to a consumption journal quantity in grams as it does to a consumption journal quantity in kilograms.
Note


If you increase the rounding precision of an item that exists on open consumption journal lines, then you must refresh the production order and at least the component need, and you must recalculate the consumption journal line.

However, if you decrease the rounding precision, then the open consumption journal line is unchanged, and you do not have to refresh the production order or recalculate the journal line. This is because it is considered more serious to consume too few components, when the original rounding precision was lower, than it is to consume too few components, when the original rounding precision was higher.

fields.name: Bin Filter
fields.tooltip:
Displays the filter that filters by bin code.
If the field contains a bin code, the values in the fields that show quantities are based only on entries with the bin code that is included in the filter.
You can enter a maximum of 10 characters, both numbers and letters. There are certain rules for how these can be combined:

Meaning
Sample Expression
Included

Equal to

A41

Entries from bin A41


Interval

1..5

Entries from bins 1 through 5


Either/or

B22|C22

Entries that from either bin B22 or bin C22


Different than

<>1

Entries from all bins except bin 1


To see the bin codes in the Bin table, choose the field.

fields.name: Variant Filter
fields.tooltip:
Displays the filter that filters by item variant.
If the field contains a variant code, the values in the fields that show quantities are based only on entries with the variant code that is included in the filter.
You can enter a maximum of 10 characters, both numbers and letters. There are certain rules for how these can be combined:

Meaning
Sample Expression
Included

Equal to

Blue

Entries with the Blue variant code


Interval

1..5

Entries with variant codes 1 through 5


Either/or

Paris|Rome

Entries with either the Paris or Rome variant code


Different than

<>AA

Entries with all variant codes, except AA


To see the variant codes in the Item Variant table, choose the field.

fields.name: Sales Unit of Measure
fields.tooltip: Specifies the unit of measure code used when you sell the item.

fields.name: Purch. Unit of Measure
fields.tooltip: Specifies the unit of measure code used when you purchase the item.

fields.name: Time Bucket
fields.tooltip: Specifies a time period that defines the recurring planning horizon used with Fixed Reorder Qty. or Maximum Qty. reordering policies.

fields.name: Res. Qty. on Req. Line
fields.tooltip:
Indicates the quantity of the item that is reserved.
Choose the field to see the reservation entries that make up the quantity.

fields.name: Reordering Policy
fields.tooltip: Specifies the reordering policy.

fields.name: Include Inventory
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the inventory quantity is included in the projected available balance when replenishment orders are calculated.

fields.name: Manufacturing Policy
fields.tooltip: Specifies if additional orders for any related components are calculated.

fields.name: Rescheduling Period
fields.tooltip: Specifies a period within which any suggestion to change a supply date always consists of a Reschedule action and never a Cancel + New action.

fields.name: Lot Accumulation Period
fields.tooltip: Specifies a period in which multiple demands are accumulated into one supply order when you use the Lot-for-Lot reordering policy.

fields.name: Dampener Period
fields.tooltip: Specifies a period of time during which you do not want the planning system to propose to reschedule existing supply orders forward. The dampener period limits the number of insignificant rescheduling of existing supply to a later date if that new date is within the dampener period. The dampener period function is only initiated if the supply can be rescheduled to a later date and not if the supply can be rescheduled to an earlier date. Accordingly, if the suggested new supply date is after the dampener period, then the rescheduling suggestion is not blocked. If the lot accumulation period is less than the dampener period, then the dampener period is dynamically set to equal the lot accumulation period. This is not shown in the value that you enter in the Dampener Period field. The last demand in the lot accumulation period is used to determine whether a potential supply date is in the dampener period. If this field is empty, then the value in the Default Dampener Period field in the Manufacturing Setup window applies. The value that you enter in the Dampener Period field must be a date formula, and one day (1D) is the shortest allowed period.

fields.name: Dampener Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies a dampener quantity to block insignificant change suggestions for an existing supply, if the change quantity is lower than the dampener quantity.

fields.name: Overflow Level
fields.tooltip: Specifies a quantity you allow projected inventory to exceed the reorder point, before the system suggests to decrease supply orders.

fields.name: Stockkeeping Unit Exists
fields.tooltip: Specifies that a stockkeeping unit exists for this item.

fields.name: Manufacturer Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a code for the manufacturer of the catalog item.

fields.name: Item Category Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the category that the item belongs to. Item categories also contain any assigned item attributes.

fields.name: Created From Nonstock Item
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the item was created from a catalog item.

fields.name: Substitutes Exist
fields.tooltip: Specifies that a substitute exists for this item.

fields.name: Qty. in Transit
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of the items that are currently in transit.

fields.name: Trans. Ord. Receipt (Qty.)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of the items that remains to be received but are not yet shipped as the difference between the Quantity and the Quantity Shipped fields.

fields.name: Trans. Ord. Shipment (Qty.)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of the items that remains to be shipped as the difference between the Quantity and the Quantity Shipped fields.

fields.name: Purchasing Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for a special procurement method, such as drop shipment.

fields.name: Qty. Assigned to ship
fields.tooltip:
Contains the number of units of the item that are being prepared for shipment.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of this field. It sums the contents of the Qty. Outstanding (Base) field on all type warehouse shipment lines for the item.
You can filter the Qty. Assigned to Ship field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain locations, dates and variants.
To see the warehouse activity lines that comprise the quantity, choose the field.

fields.name: Qty. Picked
fields.tooltip:
Contains the number of units of the item that have been registered as picked.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of this field. It sums the contents of the Qty. Picked (Base) field on all warehouse shipment lines for the item.
You can filter the Qty. Picked field so its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain locations, dates and variants.
To see the warehouse shipment lines that comprise the quantity, choose the field.

fields.name: Excluded from Cost Adjustment
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the item is excluded from the cost adjustment process.

fields.name: Item Tracking Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how serial, lot or package numbers assigned to the item are tracked in the supply chain.

fields.name: Lot Nos.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number series code that will be used when assigning lot numbers.

fields.name: Expiration Calculation
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date formula for calculating the expiration date on the item tracking line. Note: This field will be ignored if the involved item has Require Expiration Date Entry set to Yes on the Item Tracking Code page.

fields.name: Lot No. Filter
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Serial No. Filter
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: No. of Substitutes
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of substitutions that have been registered for the item.

fields.name: Warehouse Class Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the warehouse class code that defines the item.

fields.name: Special Equipment Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the equipment that warehouse employees must use when handling the item.

fields.name: Put-away Template Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the put-away template by which the program determines the most appropriate zone and bin for storage of the item after receipt.

fields.name: Put-away Unit of Measure Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the item unit of measure in which the program will put the item away.

fields.name: Phys Invt Counting Period Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the counting period that indicates how often you want to count the item in a physical inventory.

fields.name: Last Counting Period Update
fields.tooltip: Specifies the last date on which you calculated the counting period. It is updated when you use the function Calculate Counting Period.

fields.name: Last Phys. Invt. Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date on which you last posted the results of a physical inventory for the item to the item ledger.

fields.name: Use Cross-Docking
fields.tooltip: Specifies if this item can be cross-docked.

fields.name: Next Counting Start Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the starting date of the next counting period.

fields.name: Next Counting End Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ending date of the next counting period.

fields.name: Identifier Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a unique code for the item in terms that are useful for automatic data capture.

fields.name: Sales Blocked
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the item cannot be entered on sales documents, except return orders and credit memos, and journals.

fields.name: Purchasing Blocked
fields.tooltip: Specifies that transactions with the item cannot be purchased.

fields.name: Service Blocked
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the item cannot be entered on service items, service contracts and service documents, except credit memos.

fields.name: Over-Receipt Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the policy that will be used for the item if more items than ordered are received.

fields.name: Qty. on Blanket Sales Order
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item are allocated to blanket sales orders, meaning listed on outstanding blanket sales order lines.

fields.name: Qty. on Blanket Purch. Order
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many units of the item are allocated to blanket purchase orders, meaning listed on outstanding blanket purchase order lines.

fields.name: Single-Level Material Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total material cost of all the items at the first level of the item's BOM.

fields.name: Single-Level Capacity Cost
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the single-level capacity cost. The single-level capacity cost is calculated as the capacity cost of the parent item only.
The program uses this field internally.

fields.name: Single-Level Subcontrd. Cost
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the single-level cost of outsourcing operations to a subcontractor. The single-level subcontractor cost is calculated as the cost of outsourced operations of the parent item only.
The program uses this field internally.

fields.name: Single-Level Cap. Ovhd Cost
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the single-level capacity overhead cost. The single-level capacity overhead cost is calculated as overhead cost concerning capacity of the parent item only.
The program uses this field internally.

fields.name: Single-Level Mfg. Ovhd Cost
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the single-level manufacturing overhead cost. The single-level manufacturing overhead cost is calculated as the overhead cost of manufacturing the parent item only.
The program uses this field internally.

fields.name: Overhead Rate
fields.tooltip: Specifies the item's indirect cost as an absolute amount.

fields.name: Rolled-up Subcontracted Cost
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the rolled-up costs of outsourcing operations to a subcontractor.
This calculation adds up the total costs of outsourced operations in connection with producing the item on the line. This includes outsourced operations on those of its components that are production BOMs in themselves.
The program uses this field internally.

fields.name: Rolled-up Mfg. Ovhd Cost
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the rolled-up manufacturing overhead cost of the item. This is a calculation of the total manufacturing overhead cost of the components added to the manufacturing overhead cost of the parent item.
The program uses this field internally.

fields.name: Rolled-up Cap. Overhead Cost
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the rolled-up capacity overhead cost of producing the item. This is a calculation of the total capacity overhead cost of producing the components added to the capacity overhead cost of producing the parent item.
The program uses this field internally.

fields.name: Planning Issues (Qty.)
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Planning Receipt (Qty.)
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Planning Release (Qty.)
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Purch. Req. Receipt (Qty.)
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Purch. Req. Release (Qty.)
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Order Tracking Policy
fields.tooltip: Specifies if and how order tracking entries are created and maintained between supply and its corresponding demand.

fields.name: Prod. Forecast Quantity (Base)
fields.tooltip:
Shows either the item's total production forecast quantity or, if you have defined a filter in the Production Forecast Name field, a filtered production forecast quantity.
The program automatically calculates and updates the contents of the field using the entries in the Forecast Quantity field in the Production Forecast Entries window.
You can also filter the Prod. Forecast Quantity (Base) field so that its contents are based only on certain locations and forecast types. You set these filters in the production forecast header, and then the filtered quantities are displayed.
To see the production forecast entries included in the forecast, click the field.

fields.name: Production Forecast Name
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Component Forecast
fields.tooltip:
Shows the filter that filters by component.
If you enter Yes in the field, the values in the fields that contain quantities are based only on component entries.
If you enter No in the field, the values in the fields that contain quantities are based only on sales item entries.
If the field is blank, the values in the fields that contain quantities are based on component and sales item entries.

fields.name: Single-Lvl Mat. Non-Invt. Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total Non-inventory material cost of all components on the parent item's BOM

fields.name: Allow Whse. Overpick
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the record is allowed to be created in the Warehouse Pick list against the Released Production Order more than the quantity defined in the component Line. For example, system will allow to create Pick for 10 units even if the component in the BOM is defined for 3 units.

fields.name: Critical
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the item is included in availability calculations to promise a shipment date for its parent item.

fields.name: Common Item No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a commonly used number for the item.
